inputs.label: Shipment Weight
inputs.businessContext: Total weight of the shipment. Standard unit for freight cost calculation per ISO 9001 and industry practice.

inputs.label: Distance
inputs.businessContext: Transportation distance. Key driver of fuel and time costs; used in cost-per-mile models.

inputs.label: Transport Mode
inputs.businessContext: Mode of transport affects cost structure, speed, and capacity. Based on logistics industry standards (WERC).

inputs.label: Fuel Price
inputs.businessContext: Current fuel price per liter. Major variable cost component; sensitive to market fluctuations.

inputs.label: Labor Rate
inputs.businessContext: Hourly wage for drivers and handlers. Based on regional labor market data.

inputs.label: Handling Cost per kg
inputs.businessContext: Cost for loading/unloading and warehousing per kg. Derived from warehouse operations standards (WERC).

inputs.label: Insurance Rate
inputs.businessContext: Insurance premium as percentage of cargo value. Standard risk management practice.

inputs.label: Cargo Value
inputs.businessContext: Declared value of goods for insurance and customs. Required for insurance cost calculation.

inputs.label: Data Confidence
inputs.businessContext: Confidence level in input data accuracy. Used to adjust final cost for uncertainty (e.g., ±10% range).

thresholds.fuelPrice > 1.5: Fuel price is high; consider fuel surcharge or alternative routes.
thresholds.distance > 1000: Long distance; consider intermodal or rail to reduce cost.
thresholds.shipmentWeight > 20000: Heavy shipment; verify weight limits and potential oversize charges.

suggestedActions: Consider consolidating shipments to reduce cost per kg.
Evaluate alternative transport modes for long distances.
Negotiate fuel surcharge with carrier if fuel price is high.
Review insurance coverage to ensure appropriate rate.
